author: Nadirs
text:
Stand up the greenfield skeleton: next module tree + §8 dispatch

Decides the alongside structure (epic bl-72a8): the greenfield is the NEXT MAJOR VERSION of the same balls crate / bl binary, not a separate crate or a rebrand. It lives in src/next/ alongside the current modules — legacy keeps bl self-hosting while each phase lands here; at cutover (bl-0802) the legacy modules are deleted, src/next/ promotes to the crate root, and the major version bumps.

Because it is one crate, the 100%-coverage and 300-line gates already cover it: check-line-lengths.sh globs src/, tarpaulin runs the whole crate. No Makefile or script change — the harness extends for free, and the build proves it (6766/6766 covered, all files <300 lines).

Content is the §0/§8 spine only, no behavior: Verb (§9, the 14 verbs) -> OpClass (mutating vs diffless, the §8 seal/no-seal split) -> Op::phases (the five-step lifecycle for mutating ops, pre+post for diffless). next::run is the verb->op dispatch entrypoint, reachable as public API and exercised by tests; it is intentionally NOT wired to main yet (current bl stays untouched). Each later phase fills a named seam: layout (§1/§2), task/id (§3), plugin dispatch (§6/§7), commit trailers (§5), the op-phase bodies (§8/§14).
